title: [flareon] Battle Guide
summary: A [fire]-type physical breaker that runs [guts] and [facade] to weaponize burns into raw damage, backed by 130 Attack and 110 Special Defense.

110 Special Defense tanks neutral special hits comfortably

recommendedMoves.reason: Main [fire] STAB with hard-hitting base power
Doubles in power when burned, stacking with [guts]

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[flareon] is a single-type [fire] physical breaker with an unusual stat distribution. The role it fills is a [guts] and [facade] breaker that turns opposing burn attempts into damage boosts. [flareon] isn't a sweeper — its job is to come in on a single predicted turn and unload a decisive [flare-blitz] or [facade] into whichever Pokemon the team needs removed.

## Strengths
The 130 Attack stat is the highest raw attack among [fire]-types of its era, and with [guts] active it reaches an effective 195. [facade] is the defining move: when [flareon] is burned, [guts] negates the burn's Attack drop and [facade] doubles in power, producing one of the hardest single-hit moves in the game. 110 Special Defense is also an underappreciated stat that lets [flareon] absorb a stray special hit when needed.

## Weaknesses & Answers
The most limiting problem is Speed. 65 is slow enough that [flareon] almost always moves second, and any faster attacker with a Water, Ground, or Rock move revenge-kills it. The coverage pool is narrow for a pure [fire]. [flareon] also struggles against bulky Waters that don't care about the [fire] STAB and can two-shot it with scald or hydro-pump.

## Recommended Moveset
[flare-blitz] is the locked [fire] STAB for maximum damage output, though the recoil does stack up over long matches. [facade] is the [guts] payoff move. [superpower] is the coverage slot that handles Rock, Dark, and Steel walls that otherwise resist the STAB. The fourth slot is [quick-attack] as a priority revenge tool to compensate for the Speed issue.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[charcoal] is the clean-damage pick that boosts [fire] STAB with no drawbacks, fitting a four-move set nicely. [focus-sash] is an alternate option for a lead-like role, ensuring [flareon] survives long enough to use one big attack. The ability must be [guts] in the format. Tera [fire] maxes [flare-blitz] damage, while Tera [normal] specifically amplifies [facade] for the burn-abuse build.

## Teammates & Synergy
[flareon] needs Speed control to function. A Ground partner answers the Rock, Water, and Electric threats that would otherwise dominate [flareon], and a stealth-rock user chips opposing switches so [flareon]'s one big attack finishes off the target. A status remover helps prevent the opponent from removing the burn before [flareon] uses it.

## Play Patterns
[flareon] is a breaker, not a cleaner. With flame-orb the plan is to come in, take the burn self-applied, and hit [facade] for massive damage; with [charcoal] the plan is raw [flare-blitz] plus [superpower] depending on the matchup. Keep [flareon] in the back of the order so it isn't chipped by hazards or priority before its entry turn.
